heroHeadline: Netanyahu Reveals Prostate Cancer Treatment; IDF Strikes Continue Across Gaza
heroSubtitle: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu publicly disclosed he was successfully treated for prostate cancer, raising questions about health transparency and succession during the ongoing Gaza conflict. The disclosure follows a series of deadly IDF airstrikes across northern Gaza, including a strike near Al-Qassam mosque in Beit Lahia that killed five Palestinians including three children on April 23.
footerNote: Data sourced from UN OCHA, IDF, Gaza Ministry of Health, B'Tselem, ICC, ICJ, Reuters, Al Jazeera, OHCHR, UNRWA, Yesh Din, Human Rights Watch, and institutional analyses. Casualty figures are subject to ongoing verification. The conflict has persisted since Israeli independence on May 14, 1948.
